mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate signals tightening labor markets that boost consumer spending power and corporate revenue growth, driving positive equity returns within a one-week window.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite falling unemployment, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in industrial production and housing starts confirms broad-based economic expansion, validating earnings upgrade cycles and pushing the S&P 500 higher over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 1.5% over any 3-year rolling period, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth exceeding 2.5% annualized triggers a repricing of corporate profit margins as demand outpaces supply constraints, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when GDP growth exceeds the threshold, or the signal's information ratio drops below 0.2 over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent uptick in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and operational efficiency, which directly supports corporate earnings and drives positive equity performance within one week.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month Spearman correlation between INDPRO changes and 5-day SPX returns remains below 0.05, indicating no structural link.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the federal funds rate signals aggressive monetary tightening that raises borrowing costs and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal exhibits a negative Sharpe ratio over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong nonfarm payroll growth and a stagnant unemployment rate indicates labor market inefficiencies that precede wage-push inflation, forcing the Federal Reserve to tighten policy and depressing equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to predict negative equity returns, or forward returns remain statistically indistinguishable from zero across all tested subsamples.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in real personal savings and spending signals household balance sheet deterioration that will curb consumer demand, leading to earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns within a one-week window.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive despite consecutive monthly declines in RSAFS, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below 50% in out-of-sample testing.
